evals.prompt: I have USDC on Taiko and want to transact without ETH. Can you set up a wallet for my agent?
evals.expected_output: Should use standard viem/4337 tooling to interact with Servo's RPC. Derive a counterfactual address from the factory, explain funding, show the full stub→permit→final→sign→submit flow with correct Servo addresses.

assertions.name: uses_standard_tooling
assertions.description: Uses viem or another standard ERC-4337 library (not a proprietary SDK)
assertions.criteria: The response uses standard tooling like viem, permissionless.js, or raw fetch/HTTP calls to interact with the RPC — NOT a proprietary SDK like @agent-paymaster/sdk

assertions.name: correct_servo_rpc
assertions.description: Uses the correct Servo RPC URL

assertions.name: explains_counterfactual
assertions.description: Explains deterministic address derivation and USDC funding before deployment
assertions.criteria: The response explains that the wallet address is derived before deployment (CREATE2/counterfactual) and that USDC should be sent to this address

assertions.name: no_eth_required
assertions.description: Makes clear no ETH is needed
assertions.criteria: The response clearly states no ETH is needed — not for gas, deployment, or anything

assertions.name: shows_two_step_quote
assertions.criteria: The response demonstrates calling pm_getPaymasterStubData first (to learn the USDC cost), then signing a permit, then calling pm_getPaymasterData with the permit

evals.prompt: I want my agent to have a smart wallet on Taiko and transact with USDC only. Can you set it up? It should be able to call any contract.
evals.expected_output: Should show how to derive the wallet, encode arbitrary calls via execute(), batch calls via executeBatch(), and interact with Servo's paymaster RPC — all with standard tooling.

assertions.name: uses_standard_tooling
assertions.description: Uses viem or standard 4337 tooling, not a proprietary SDK
assertions.criteria: Uses viem, permissionless.js, or raw HTTP/fetch — NOT @agent-paymaster/sdk

assertions.name: shows_arbitrary_call
assertions.description: Shows how to call any contract with arbitrary calldata
assertions.criteria: Demonstrates encoding and sending a call to an arbitrary contract, making clear the agent can interact with any contract

assertions.name: mentions_batch
assertions.criteria: Mentions or demonstrates that multiple calls can be batched atomically

assertions.name: explains_usdc_gas
assertions.description: Explains gas is paid in USDC automatically via the paymaster
assertions.criteria: Explains that gas fees are paid in USDC through the paymaster, not ETH

evals.prompt: How much does it cost to send a tx through Servo? My agent is kinda slow, it thinks for a few minutes before actually sending.
evals.expected_output: Should explain pricing (5% surcharge), warn about the 90s quote TTL, and advise the two-phase pattern.

assertions.name: mentions_90s_ttl
assertions.criteria: Warns that paymaster quotes expire after about 90 seconds and this is a problem for a slow agent

assertions.name: advises_two_phase
assertions.description: Recommends separating price check from submission
assertions.criteria: Advises: check the price first (take your time), then get a fresh quote and submit quickly when ready

assertions.name: mentions_pricing
assertions.criteria: Mentions the 5% surcharge on gas costs
